commands.repair_reason: - [P1] This PR needs contributor proof and maintainer dedupe against sibling candidate fixes, not an automated repair branch.; Cleared: The diff only changes session metadata merge logic and a colocated Vitest file; no concrete security or supply-chain concern was found. (sha=fb86df4807b217126cb183db5833a202ef28d63f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR clears `nativeChannelId`, `nativeDirectUserId`, and `threadId` from session origin metadata when provider or surface changes, and adds focused regression tests for the channel-switch merge path.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +116. Total +129 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current `mergeOrigin` preserves old channel-keyed origin fields when the next channel omits them, and Slack can supply `nativeChannelId` while Telegram direct contexts omit it. I did not run a live Slack-to-Telegram transport repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs contributor proof and maintainer dedupe against sibling candidate fixes, not an automated repair branch.

target.title: fix(sessions): reset stale per-channel origin fields on channel switch (#95325)
target.body:
## Summary

With `session.dmScope: "main"`, a user's DMs across channels (Slack, Telegram, webchat) share one session. When the channel switches (e.g. Slack â†’ Telegram), the session origin's per-channel identity fields were not reset.

`mergeOrigin` only overwrites a field when the new inbound supplies it. Telegram DMs do not supply `nativeChannelId` (or `threadId`), so the previous Slack channel id persisted in the now-Telegram session and kept persisting on every subsequent Telegram turn until the user messaged on the original channel again.

## Changes

- `src/config/sessions/metadata.ts`: Detect a `provider` or `surface` change in `mergeOrigin` and reset the channel-specific fields (`nativeChannelId`, `nativeDirectUserId`, `threadId`) before applying the new channel's values. Same-channel turns that keep supplying `nativeChannelId` continue to update it as before.
- `src/config/sessions/metadata.test.ts`: Add regression tests covering the channel-switch reset and the preserved-same-channel case.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Session origin no longer retains the previous channel's `nativeChannelId`/`nativeDirectUserId`/`threadId` after a provider or surface change.

**Real environment tested**: Windows 10, Node.js v22.19.2, source tree at commit fb86df4807.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/config/sessions/metadata.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
   Duration  15.70s
```

The four cases exercised:
1. Slack â†’ Telegram switch with Telegram supplying no `nativeChannelId`: stale Slack `nativeChannelId`/`nativeDirectUserId`/`threadId` are now undefined after the switch.
2. Same-channel Slack turn keeps supplying `nativeChannelId`: value updates normally (`Dslackdm1` â†’ `Dslackdm2`).
3. Telegram â†’ Slack switch where Slack supplies `nativeChannelId`: new `Dnewslack` is adopted, stale Telegram `nativeDirectUserId` is reset.
4. Surface change (`slack` â†’ `slack-im`) with matching provider still resets channel-specific fields.

**Observed result after fix**: After the fix, switching channels clears the stale prior-channel native identity; previously the Slack channel id remained in the Telegram session.

**What was not tested**: Live Slackâ†”Telegram round-trip over real channels (requires live credentials). The fix is unit-level and driven by `deriveSessionMetaPatch`, which is the single merge path the runtime uses.

## Verification

- New regression tests: 4/4 passing.
- Pre-existing `sessionFile` path tests (2) fail identically on base `origin/main` without this change â€” unrelated to origin merging.
- `tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json`: no errors in the modified file.
- `oxlint` on both files: clean.

Fixes #95325
target.author: ruomuxydt
